USD/PHP FX Risk Analysis | Items 1-6

  • Section 1: Data Load and Integrity Check
  • Section 2: Data Cleaning
  • Section 3: Window Definitions
  • Section 4: Item 1 Tables (DoD VaR 95%)
  • Section 5: Item 2 Tables (Intraday VaR 95%)
  • Section 6: Item 3 Table (Gap Analysis)
  • Section 7: Item 4 Table (CVaR & Winsorization)
  • Section 8: Item 5 Tables (Significance)
  • Section 9: Item 6 Table (Portfolio)
  • Section 10: Summary Table
  • Section 11: Strategic Risk Recommendations
    • 1. Ditch VaR for CVaR
    • 2. Trade Dollars for Pesos
    • 3. Face the Price of Safety

Section 1: Data Load and Integrity Check

df_raw <- read_csv("data/unified_usdphp_bap.csv") %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) %>%
  # Filter for trading days only (Monday=2, ..., Friday=6)
  # This removes the weekends imputed by the consolidation script
  filter(wday(Date) %in% 2:6) %>%
  # Fix specific outlier on 2021-05-20: 478.855 -> 47.855
  mutate(Open = ifelse(Date == as.Date("2021-05-20") & Open == 478.855, 47.855, Open)) %>%
  arrange(Date)

integrity_check <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Total Trading Row Count", "Start Date", "End Date", "Rows with High < Low"),
  Value = c(
    as.character(nrow(df_raw)),
    as.character(min(df_raw$Date)),
    as.character(max(df_raw$Date)),
    as.character(nrow(filter(df_raw, High < Low)))
  )
)
kable(integrity_check, caption = "Data Integrity Check")
Data Integrity Check
Metric Value
Total Trading Row Count 1869
Start Date 2019-01-01
End Date 2026-02-27
Rows with High < Low 0

Section 2: Data Cleaning

# Swap High/Low errors
df <- df_raw %>%
  mutate(
    ActualHigh = pmax(High, Low),
    ActualLow = pmin(High, Low),
    High = ActualHigh,
    Low = ActualLow
  ) %>%
  select(-ActualHigh, -ActualLow)

# Compute Derived Columns
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    dod_cents = (Close - lag(Close)) * 100,
    dod_pct = (Close - lag(Close)) / lag(Close) * 100,
    intraday_cents = (High - Low) * 100,
    intraday_pct = (High - Low) / Low * 100
  )

# Separate for DoD analysis (drop NA row)
df_returns <- df %>% filter(!is.na(dod_cents))

# Cleaning Verification Output
cleaning_stats <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Swapped High/Low Count", "Derived Columns", "Remaining Trading Rows"),
  Result = c(
    as.character(nrow(filter(df_raw, High < Low))),
    "DoD (cents/%), Intraday (cents/%)",
    as.character(nrow(df_returns))
  )
)
kable(cleaning_stats, caption = "Data Cleaning Results Summary")
Data Cleaning Results Summary
Metric Result
Swapped High/Low Count 0
Derived Columns DoD (cents/%), Intraday (cents/%)
Remaining Trading Rows 1868

Section 3: Window Definitions

# Applied to return series
windows_returns <- list(
  Full = df_returns,
  w60 = tail(df_returns, 60),
  w40 = tail(df_returns, 40),
  w20 = tail(df_returns, 20)
)

# Applied to intraday series
windows_intraday <- list(
  Full = df,
  w60 = tail(df, 60),
  w40 = tail(df, 40),
  w20 = tail(df, 20)
)

window_summary <- data.frame(
  Window = names(windows_returns),
  Start_Date = sapply(windows_returns, function(x) as.character(min(x$Date))),
  n = sapply(windows_returns, nrow)
)
kable(window_summary, caption = "Analysis Windows (Return Series)")
Analysis Windows (Return Series)
Window Start_Date n
Full Full 2019-01-02 1868
w60 w60 2025-12-08 60
w40 w40 2026-01-05 40
w20 w20 2026-02-02 20

Section 4: Item 1 Tables (DoD VaR 95%)

item1_var <- bind_rows(lapply(windows_returns, function(w) {
  data.frame(
    n = nrow(w),
    # VaR as absolute loss magnitude
    VaR_Pct = abs(quantile(w$dod_pct, 0.05, type = 7)),
    VaR_Cents = abs(quantile(w$dod_cents, 0.05, type = 7))
  )
}), .id = "Window")
kable(item1_var, caption = "Item 1: Close-to-Close VaR at 95% (Loss Magnitude)")
Item 1: Close-to-Close VaR at 95% (Loss Magnitude)
Window n VaR_Pct VaR_Cents
5%…1 Full 1868 0.5119062 28.000
5%…2 w60 60 0.4781771 28.200
5%…3 w40 40 0.4802720 28.325
5%…4 w20 20 0.5005179 29.475 
# Outliers
lb_999 <- quantile(df_returns$dod_pct, 0.001, type = 7)
ub_999 <- quantile(df_returns$dod_pct, 0.999, type = 7)
outliers_1 <- df_returns %>% 
  filter(dod_pct < lb_999 | dod_pct > ub_999) %>%
  select(Date, Close, dod_pct, dod_cents)
kable(outliers_1, caption = "Item 1: Outliers (0.1% / 99.9% Bounds)")
Item 1: Outliers (0.1% / 99.9% Bounds)
Date Close dod_pct dod_cents
2022-11-11 57.23 -1.649768 -96
2022-12-27 55.90 1.359927 75
2023-02-06 54.39 1.322653 71
2025-01-02 57.91 -1.830819 -108 
# --- PLOT 1: The Long Tail of Risk ---
ggplot(df_returns, aes(x = dod_pct)) +
  stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = mean(df_returns$dod_pct), sd = sd(df_returns$dod_pct)), 
                linetype = "dashed", color = econ_grey, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_density(fill = econ_blue, alpha = 0.2, color = econ_blue) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -item1_var$VaR_Pct[1], color = econ_blue, linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(x = -item1_var$VaR_Pct[1], y = 0.2, label = paste("VaR 95%:", round(item1_var$VaR_Pct[1], 3), "%")), 
            angle = 90, vjust = -1, size = 3, color = econ_blue) +
  labs(title = "The long tail of risk",
       subtitle = "Density of USD/PHP daily percentage changes vs. Normal Distribution (grey dashed), 2019-2026",
       x = "Daily Change (%)", y = "Density",
       caption = "Source: BAP Historical Data. VaR calculated using historical simulation (type 7).") +
  theme_economist_quant()

Section 5: Item 2 Tables (Intraday VaR 95%)

item2_var <- bind_rows(lapply(windows_intraday, function(w) {
  data.frame(
    n = nrow(w),
    VaR_Cents = quantile(w$intraday_cents, 0.95, type = 7),
    VaR_Pct = quantile(w$intraday_pct, 0.95, type = 7)
  )
}), .id = "Window")
kable(item2_var, caption = "Item 2: Intraday VaR at 95% (95th percentile of Range)")
Item 2: Intraday VaR at 95% (95th percentile of Range)
Window n VaR_Cents VaR_Pct
95%…1 Full 1869 40.000 0.7347808
95%…2 w60 60 28.050 0.4765687
95%…3 w40 40 29.400 0.4990832
95%…4 w20 20 37.495 0.6528108 
# Outliers
ub_intraday <- quantile(df$intraday_cents, 0.999, type = 7)
outliers_2 <- df %>% 
  filter(intraday_cents > ub_intraday) %>%
  select(Date, High, Low, intraday_cents, intraday_pct)
kable(outliers_2, caption = "Item 2: Outliers (99.9% Bound)")
Item 2: Outliers (99.9% Bound)
Date High Low intraday_cents intraday_pct
2019-09-04 52.93 51.92 101 1.945301
2024-09-12 56.20 55.08 112 2.033406

Section 6: Item 3 Table (Gap Analysis)

gap_table <- item1_var %>%
  select(Window, dod_var = VaR_Cents) %>%
  left_join(item2_var %>% select(Window, intra_var = VaR_Cents), by = "Window") %>%
  mutate(Gap = abs(intra_var) - abs(dod_var))
kable(gap_table, caption = "Item 3: VaR Gap (Intraday - DoD)")
Item 3: VaR Gap (Intraday - DoD)
Window dod_var intra_var Gap
Full 28.000 40.000 12.000
w60 28.200 28.050 -0.150
w40 28.325 29.400 1.075
w20 29.475 37.495 8.020 
# --- PLOT 2: Mind the Gap ---
# Filter last 250 days for visibility
df_recent <- tail(df, 250)

ggplot(df_recent, aes(x = Date)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = intraday_pct, color = "Intraday Range"), linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = abs(dod_pct), color = "Absolute DoD Change"), linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Intraday Range" = econ_blue, "Absolute DoD Change" = econ_red)) +
  labs(title = "Mind the gap",
       subtitle = "Intraday volatility (%) vs. Absolute Close-to-Close changes (%), trailing 250 days",
       x = NULL, y = "Magnitude (%)",
       caption = "Large gaps indicate intraday chaos that 'recovers' by the closing bell.") +
  theme_economist_quant()

Section 7: Item 4 Table (CVaR & Winsorization)

# CVaR (Expected Shortfall)
# Threshold is the 5th percentile return
threshold_pct <- -item1_var$VaR_Pct[1]
threshold_cents <- -item1_var$VaR_Cents[1]

cvar_pct <- abs(mean(df_returns$dod_pct[df_returns$dod_pct <= threshold_pct]))
cvar_cents <- abs(mean(df_returns$dod_cents[df_returns$dod_cents <= threshold_cents]))

# Winsorization
lb_999 <- quantile(df_returns$dod_pct, 0.001, type = 7)
ub_999 <- quantile(df_returns$dod_pct, 0.999, type = 7)

df_winsor <- df_returns %>%
  mutate(dod_pct_w = pmin(pmax(dod_pct, lb_999), ub_999),
         dod_cents_w = pmin(pmax(dod_cents, quantile(dod_cents, 0.001, type=7)), quantile(dod_cents, 0.999, type=7)))

var_winsor_pct <- abs(quantile(df_winsor$dod_pct_w, 0.05, type = 7))
var_winsor_cents <- abs(quantile(df_winsor$dod_cents_w, 0.05, type = 7))

item4_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Raw VaR (95%)", "CVaR (Expected Shortfall)", "Winsorization LB (0.1%)", "Winsorization UB (99.9%)", "VaR after Winsorization", "VaR reduction"),
  Percentage = c(item1_var$VaR_Pct[1], cvar_pct, lb_999, ub_999, var_winsor_pct, item1_var$VaR_Pct[1] - var_winsor_pct),
  Cents = c(item1_var$VaR_Cents[1], cvar_cents, NA, NA, var_winsor_cents, item1_var$VaR_Cents[1] - var_winsor_cents)
)
kable(item4_table, caption = "Item 4: Results (Full History)")
Item 4: Results (Full History)
Metric Percentage Cents
Raw VaR (95%) 0.5119062 28.00000
CVaR (Expected Shortfall) 0.7144078 39.74894
Winsorization LB (0.1%) -1.4943095 NA
Winsorization UB (99.9%) 1.2901539 NA
VaR after Winsorization 0.5119062 28.00000
VaR reduction 0.0000000 0.00000

Section 8: Item 5 Tables (Significance)

full_pct <- df_returns$dod_pct
item5_tests <- bind_rows(lapply(windows_returns[-1], function(w) {
  ks <- ks.test(full_pct, w$dod_pct)
  levene <- levene_test_manual(full_pct, w$dod_pct)
  data.frame(n = nrow(w), KS_Stat = ks$statistic, KS_p = ks$p.value, Levene_Stat = abs(levene$statistic), Levene_p = levene$p_value)
}), .id = "Window")

item5_desc <- bind_rows(lapply(windows_returns, function(w) {
  data.frame(n = nrow(w), Mean_Pct = mean(w$dod_pct), SD_Pct = sd(w$dod_pct), Skewness = skewness(w$dod_pct), Kurtosis = kurtosis(w$dod_pct))
}), .id = "Window")

item5_comp <- item1_var %>%
  mutate(Diff_from_Full = VaR_Pct - VaR_Pct[1])

kable(item5_tests, caption = "Item 5: Significance Tests")
Item 5: Significance Tests
Window n KS_Stat KS_p Levene_Stat Levene_p
D…1 w60 60 0.1008922 0.5948778 1.0579610 0.2902059
D…2 w40 40 0.1275161 0.5474262 0.1148780 0.9085539
D…3 w20 20 0.2016060 0.3971375 0.2491313 0.8032863 
kable(item5_desc, caption = "Item 5: Descriptive Statistics")

Window n Mean_Pct SD_Pct Skewness Kurtosis

w60 60 -0.0359482 0.2677226 -0.8181744 1.3171015

w20 20 -0.1020296 0.3155756 -0.9613373 0.7007972 
kable(item5_comp, caption = "Item 5: VaR Comparison")
Item 5: VaR Comparison
Window n VaR_Pct VaR_Cents Diff_from_Full
5%…1 Full 1868 0.5119062 28.000 0.0000000
5%…2 w60 60 0.4781771 28.200 -0.0337291
5%…3 w40 40 0.4802720 28.325 -0.0316341
5%…4 w20 20 0.5005179 29.475 -0.0113883 
# --- PLOT 3: Stability in Numbers ---
ggplot(item1_var, aes(x = Window, y = VaR_Pct, fill = Window == "Full")) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = econ_blue, "FALSE" = econ_grey)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(round(VaR_Pct, 3), "%")), vjust = 1.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  labs(title = "Stability in numbers",
       subtitle = "VaR (95%) loss magnitude across windows. Differences from historical are statistically examined.",
       x = NULL, y = "VaR Magnitude (%)",
       caption = "Blue bar represents historical baseline since 2019.") +
  theme_economist_quant() + theme(legend.position = "none")

Section 9: Item 6 Table (Portfolio)

ref_row <- df %>% filter(Date <= as.Date("2024-06-30")) %>% slice_tail(n = 1)
fx_rate <- ref_row$Close
ref_date_used <- ref_row$Date

# VaR at 99% level of confidence (1st percentile return)
var_99_pct <- abs(quantile(df_returns$dod_pct, 0.01, type = 7))
upside_99_pct <- quantile(df_returns$dod_pct, 0.99, type = 7)

pos_usd <- 10000000
loss_usd <- (var_99_pct / 100) * pos_usd
loss_php <- loss_usd * fx_rate

target_php <- 20000
reduced_pos_usd <- target_php / ((var_99_pct / 100) * fx_rate)
upside_full_php <- (upside_99_pct / 100) * pos_usd * fx_rate
upside_reduced_php <- (upside_99_pct / 100) * reduced_pos_usd * fx_rate
upside_sacrificed_php <- upside_full_php - upside_reduced_php

item6_table <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c("Reference Date Used", "FX Rate (PHP/USD)", "VaR at 99% (%)", "USD Position", "1-Day Loss (USD)", "1-Day Loss (PHP)", "Target Max Loss (PHP)", "Reduced USD Position", "USD to Remove", "Upside Return 99% (%)", "Upside Full (PHP)", "Upside Reduced (PHP)", "Upside Sacrificed (PHP)"),
  Value = c(as.character(ref_date_used), fx_rate, var_99_pct, pos_usd, loss_usd, loss_php, target_php, reduced_pos_usd, pos_usd - reduced_pos_usd, upside_99_pct, upside_full_php, upside_reduced_php, upside_sacrificed_php)
)
kable(item6_table, caption = "Item 6: Portfolio Loss and Hedge Sizing")
Item 6: Portfolio Loss and Hedge Sizing
Parameter Value
Reference Date Used 2024-06-28
FX Rate (PHP/USD) 58.61
VaR at 99% (%) 0.790657883722102
USD Position 1e+07
1-Day Loss (USD) 79065.7883722102
1-Day Loss (PHP) 4634045.85649524
Target Max Loss (PHP) 20000
Reduced USD Position 43158.8305755915
USD to Remove 9956841.16942441
Upside Return 99% (%) 0.897626213370316
Upside Full (PHP) 5260987.23656342
Upside Reduced (PHP) 22705.805680319
Upside Sacrificed (PHP) 5238281.4308831 
# --- PLOT 4: The Price of Safety ---
port_viz <- data.frame(
  Category = c("Full Exposure\n(PHP 10M)", "Capped Loss\n(Target)"),
  Loss = c(loss_php / 1e6, target_php / 1e6),
  Upside = c(upside_full_php / 1e6, upside_reduced_php / 1e6)
)

ggplot(port_viz, aes(x = Category)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = -Loss, fill = "1-Day Loss (99%)"), width = 0.5) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Upside, fill = "99th Percentile Upside"), width = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1-Day Loss (99%)" = econ_red, "99th Percentile Upside" = econ_blue)) +
  labs(title = "The price of safety",
       subtitle = "Potential 1-day loss vs. upside potential in millions of PHP (Reference: June 2024)",
       x = NULL, y = "PHP (Millions)",
       caption = "Capping loss at PHP 20,000 virtually eliminates potential upside potential.") +
  theme_economist_quant()

Section 10: Summary Table

summary_table <- data.frame(
  Item = c(1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 5, 5, 6, 6, 6),
  Description = c("Close-to-close VaR 95% full", "Outlier count (DoD)", "Intraday VaR 95% full", "Outlier count (Intraday)", "VaR gap full", "CVaR 95% full", "Winsorization adjustment", "KS test p-values (60,40,20)", "Max VaR diff across windows", "1-day loss USD 10M (PHP)", "USD pos to cap at PHP 20K", "Upside sacrificed"),
  Result = c(
    paste(round(item1_var$VaR_Cents[1], 2), "cents /", round(item1_var$VaR_Pct[1], 4), "%"),
    as.character(nrow(outliers_1)),
    paste(round(item2_var$VaR_Cents[1], 2), "cents /", round(item2_var$VaR_Pct[1], 4), "%"),
    as.character(nrow(outliers_2)),
    paste(round(gap_table$Gap[1], 2), "cents"),
    paste(round(cvar_cents, 2), "cents /", round(cvar_pct, 4), "%"),
    paste(round(item1_var$VaR_Pct[1] - var_winsor_pct, 4), "pp"),
    paste(round(item5_tests$KS_p, 4), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(max(abs(item5_comp$Diff_from_Full)), 4), "pp"),
    paste("PHP", format(round(loss_php, 2), big.mark=",")),
    paste("USD", format(round(reduced_pos_usd, 2), big.mark=",")),
    paste("PHP", format(round(upside_sacrificed_php, 2), big.mark=","))
  )
)
kable(summary_table, caption = "Final Summary Table")
Final Summary Table
Item Description Result

Section 11: Strategic Risk Recommendations

Based on the quantitative analysis of USD/PHP data (2019-2026), these four steps will improve the firm’s risk posture:

1. Ditch VaR for CVaR

Value at Risk (VaR) is a flawed metric. It is not “coherent” because it fails the test of subadditivity: it can wrongly suggest that a diversified portfolio is riskier than the sum of its parts. The institution should switch to Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR). Unlike VaR, which ignores the “tail,” CVaR accounts for the worst-case scenarios and behaves logically when assets are combined.

2. Trade Dollars for Pesos

To cap daily losses at PHP 20,000, move capital out of dollars and into PHP cash or T-bills. This exploits the Translation Invariance of coherent risk measures: moving assets into the base currency reduces total risk \(\rho(X)\) by exactly the amount moved. It is a mathematical certainty, not a forecast.

3. Face the Price of Safety

Risk management is never a free lunch. Capping one-day losses at PHP 20,000 requires gutting the dollar position so severely that it eliminates potential gains. This safety comes at an opportunity cost of approximately PHP 5.4m in 99th-percentile upside. Managers must decide if avoiding a small daily loss is worth sacrificing millions in potential profit.
